Replaces round-robin with latency-aware selection using EWMA-smoothed\n4\t//! metrics: latency p95, in-flight request count, and error rate.\n5\t\n6\tuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\n7\tuse \"snake_case\")]\n18\tpub enum SelectionStrategy {\n19\t    /// EWMA-based adaptive selection.\n20\t    Adaptive,\n21\t    /// Round-robin selection.\n22\t    RoundRobin,\n23\t    /// Random selection.\n24\t    Random,\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\timpl Default for SelectionStrategy {\n28\t    fn default() -> Self {\n29\t        Self::Adaptive\n30\t    }\n31\t}\n32\t\n33\t/// Replica selection #[serde(default = \"default_inflight_weight\")]\n44\t    pub inflight_weight: f64,\n45\t    /// Error rate weight.\n46\t    #[serde(default = \"default_error_weight\")]\n47\t    pub error_weight: default_inflight_weight() -> f64 {\n60\t    2.0\n61\t}\n62\tfn default_error_weight() -> f64 {\n63\t    10.0\n64\t}\n65\tfn default_ewma_half_life() -> u64 {\n66\t    5000\n67\t}\n68\tfn default_epsilon() -> f64 {\n69\t    0.05\n70\t}\n71\t\n72\timpl Default for ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n73\t    fn default() -> Self {\n74\t        Self {\n75\t            strategy: \"adaptive\".into(),\n76\t            latency_weight: default_latency_weight(),\n77\t            inflight_weight: default_inflight_weight(),\n78\t            error_weight: default_error_weight(),\n79\t            ewma_half_life_ms: default_ewma_half_life(),\n80\t            exploration_epsilon: default_epsilon(),\n81\t        }\n82\t    }\n83\t}\n84\t\n85\t/// Per-node metrics for adaptive selection.\n86\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n87\tpub struct NodeMetrics {\n88\t    /// EWMA of latency p95 in milliseconds.\n89\t    pub latency_p95_ms: f64,\n90\t    /// Current in-flight request count.\n91\t    pub in_flight: u32,\n92\t    /// EWMA of error rate (0.0 to 1.0).\n93\t    pub error_rate: f64,\n94\t    /// EWMA half-life for updates.\n95\t    pub half_life_ms: u64,\n96\t    /// Last update timestamp.\n97\t    pub last_updated: Instant,\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\timpl NodeMetrics {\n101\t    /// Create new metrics with initial values.\n102\t    pub fn new(initial_latency_ms: f64, half_life_ms: u64) -> Self {\n103\t        Self {\n104\t            latency_p95_ms: initial_latency_ms,\n105\t            in_flight: 0,\n106\t            error_rate: 0.0,\n107\t            half_life_ms,\n108\t            last_updated: Instant::now(),\n109\t        }\n110\t    }\n111\t\n112\t    /// Update latency with EWMA smoothing.\n113\t    pub fn update_latency(&mut self, latency_ms: f64) {\n114\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n115\t        self.latency_p95_ms = alpha * self.latency_p95_ms + (1.0 - alpha) * latency_ms;\n116\t        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    /// Update error rate with EWMA smoothing.\n120\t    pub fn update_error(&mut self, is_error: bool) {\n121\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n122\t        let new_error = if is_error { 1.0 } else { 0.0 };\n123\t        self.error_rate = alpha * self.error_rate + (1.0 - alpha) * new_error;\n124\t        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n125\t    }\n126\t\n127\t    /// Increment in-flight count.\n128\t    pub fn increment_in_flight(&mut self) {\n129\t        self.in_flight += 1;\n130\t    }\n131\t\n132\t    /// Decrement in-flight count.\n133\t    pub fn decrement_in_flight(&mut self) {\n134\t        self.in_flight = self.in_flight.saturating_sub(1);\n135\t    }\n136\t\n137\t    /// Compute the composite score (lower is better).\n138\t    pub fn score(&self, config: &ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> f64 {\n139\t        config.latency_weight * self.latency_p95_ms\n140\t            + config.inflight_weight * (self.in_flight as f64)\n141\t            + config.error_weight * (self.error_rate * 1000.0)\n142\t    }\n143\t}\n144\t\n145\timpl Default for NodeMetrics {\n146\t    fn default() -> Self {\n147\t        Self::new(50.0, 5000)\n148\t    }\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t/// Callback for reporting selection events.\n152\tpub trait SelectionObserver: Send + Sync {\n153\t    /// Called when a node is selected with its score.\n154\t    fn report_selection(&self, node_id: &str, score: f64);\n155\t    /// Called when exploration selects a random node.\n156\t    fn report_exploration(&self);\n157\t}\n158\t\n159\t/// No-op observer for when metrics aren't needed.\n160\tstruct NoOpObserver;\n161\timpl SelectionObserver for NoOpObserver {\n162\t    fn report_selection(&self, _node_id: &str, _score: f64) {}\n163\t    fn report_exploration(&self) {}\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\t/// Replica selector.\n167\tpub struct ReplicaSelector {\n168\t    /// Configuration.\n169\t    config: ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n170\t    /// Per-node metrics.\n171\t    metrics: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<NodeId, NodeMetrics>>>,\n172\t    /// Round-robin counter (for round-robin strategy).\n173\t    rr_counter: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, u64>>>,\n174\t    /// Random number generator.\n175\t    rng: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<StdRng>>,\n176\t    /// Observer for selection events.\n177\t    observer: Arc<dyn SelectionObserver>,\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\timpl ReplicaSelector {\n181\t    /// Create a new replica selector with a metrics observer.\n182\t    pub fn new_with_observer(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig, observer: Arc<dyn SelectionObserver>) -> Self {\n183\t        Self {\n184\t            config,\n185\t            metrics: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n186\t            rr_counter: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n187\t            rng: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(StdRng::from_entropy())),\n188\t            observer,\n189\t        }\n190\t    }\n191\t\n192\t    /// Create a new replica selector without metrics.\n193\t    pub fn new(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> Self {\n194\t        Self::new_with_observer(config, Arc::new(NoOpObserver))\n195\t    }\n196\t\n197\t    /// Select a node from the given candidates.\n198\t    ///\n199\t    /// Returns the selected node ID, or None if candidates is empty.\n200\t    pub async fn select(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u32) -> Option<NodeId> {\n201\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n202\t            return None;\n203\t        }\n204\t\n205\t        let strategy = self.parse_strategy();\n206\t\n207\t        match strategy {\n208\t            SelectionStrategy::Adaptive => self.select_adaptive(candidates).await,\n209\t            SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin => self.select_round_robin(candidates, group_id as u64).await,\n210\t            SelectionStrategy::Random => self.select_random(candidates),\n211\t        }\n212\t    }\n213\t\n214\t    /// Adaptive selection using EWMA scores.\n215\t    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n216\t        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n217\t\n218\t        // Exploration: with probability epsilon, pick randomly\n219\t        if self.should_explore() {\n220\t            self.observer.report_exploration();\n221\t            let selected = self.select_random(candidates);\n222\t            if let Some(ref node) = selected {\n223\t                let score = metrics\n224\t                    .get(node)\n225\t                    .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n226\t                    .unwrap_or(1000.0);\n227\t                self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), score);\n228\t            }\n229\t            return selected;\n230\t        }\n231\t\n232\t        // Compute scores and collect all nodes with the minimum score\n233\t        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;\n234\t        let mut best_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = Vec::new();\n235\t\n236\t        for node in candidates {\n237\t            let score = metrics\n238\t                .get(node)\n239\t                .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n240\t                .unwrap_or(1000.0); // High default for unknown nodes\n241\t\n242\t            if score < best_score {\n243\t                best_score = score;\n244\t                best_nodes.clear();\n245\t                best_nodes.push(node.clone());\n246\t            } else if (score - best_score).abs() < 1e-10 {\n247\t                // Scores are essentially equal - add to tie list\n248\t                best_nodes.push(node.clone());\n249\t            }\n250\t        }\n251\t\n252\t        // If multiple nodes have the same best score, pick randomly\n253\t        let selected = if best_nodes.len() == 1 {\n254\t            best_nodes.into_iter().next()\n255\t        } else {\n256\t            let idx = self.rng.lock().unwrap().gen_range(0..best_nodes.len());\n257\t            best_nodes.get(idx).cloned()\n258\t        };\n259\t\n260\t        if let Some(ref node) = selected {\n261\t            self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), best_score);\n262\t        }\n263\t\n264\t        selected\n265\t    }\n266\t\n267\t    /// Round-robin selection.\n268\t    async fn select_round_robin(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u64) -> Option<NodeId> {\n269\t        let key = format!(\"group_{}\", group_id);\n270\t        let mut counter = self.rr_counter.write().await;\n271\t        let idx = *counter.entry(key.clone()).or_insert(0) as usize % candidates.len();\n272\t        *counter.get_mut(&key).unwrap() += 1;\n273\t        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n274\t    }\n275\t\n276\t    /// Random selection.\n277\t    fn select_random(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n278\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n279\t            return None;\n280\t        }\n281\t        let idx = self\n282\t            .rng\n283\t            .lock()\n284\t            .unwrap()\n285\t            .gen_range(0..candidates.len());\n286\t        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n287\t    }\n288\t\n289\t    /// Check if we should explore (random selection).\n290\t    fn should_explore(&self) -> bool {\n291\t        let mut rng = self.rng.lock().unwrap();\n292\t        rng.gen::<f64>() < self.config.exploration_epsilon\n293\t    }\n294\t\n295\t    /// Record a successful request (update latency).\n296\t    pub async fn record_success(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: f64) {\n297\t        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n298\t        let entry = metrics\n299\t            .entry(node.clone())\n300\t            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n301\t        entry.update_latency(latency_ms);\n302\t        entry.update_error(false);\n303\t        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n304\t    }\n305\t\n306\t    /// Record a failed request.\n307\t    pub async fn record_error(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: Option<f64>) {\n308\t        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n309\t        let entry = metrics\n310\t            .entry(node.clone())\n311\t            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n312\t        if let Some(lat) = latency_ms {\n313\t            entry.update_latency(lat);\n314\t        }\n315\t        entry.update_error(true);\n316\t        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n317\t    }\n318\t\n319\t    /// Record that a request is being sent to a node.\n320\t    pub async fn record_request_start(&self, node: &NodeId) {\n321\t        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n322\t        let entry = metrics\n323\t            .entry(node.clone())\n324\t            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n325\t        entry.increment_in_flight();\n326\t    }\n327\t\n328\t    /// Get metrics for a node.\n329\t    pub async fn get_metrics(&self, node: &NodeId) -> Option<NodeMetrics> {\n330\t        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n331\t        metrics.get(node).cloned()\n332\t    }\n333\t\n334\t    /// Parse the strategy from config string.\n335\t    fn parse_strategy(&self) -> SelectionStrategy {\n336\t        match self.config.strategy.as_str() {\n337\t            \"round_robin\" => SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin,\n338\t            \"random\" => SelectionStrategy::Random,\n339\t            _ => SelectionStrategy::Adaptive,\n340\t        }\n341\t    }\n342\t}\n343\t\n344\timpl Clone for ReplicaSelector {\n345\t    fn clone(&self) -> Self {\n346\t        Self {\n347\t            config: self.config.clone(),\n348\t            metrics:   }\n353\t    }\n354\t}\n355\t\n356\timpl Default for ReplicaSelector {\n357\t    fn default() -> Self {\n358\t        Self::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n359\t    }\n360\t}\n361\t\n362\t/// {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_01f5ac59602646ceadc76236","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::migration::{MigrationCoordinator, ShardId};\n4\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n5\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n6\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n7\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n8\t\n9\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n10\t///\n11\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n12\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n13\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n14\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n15\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n16\t    h.finish()\n17\t}\n18\t\n19\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n20\t///\n21\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// Sorts by score descending, breaking ties lexicographically on node_id\n24\t/// for deterministic resolution.\n25\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n26\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n27\t        .iter()\n28\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n29\t        .collect();\n30\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n31\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n32\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n33\t    });\n34\t    scored\n35\t        .into_iter()\n36\t        .take(rf)\n37\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n38\t        .collect()\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n42\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n43\t    topology\n44\t        .groups()\n45\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n46\t        .collect()\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t/// All write targets for a document, considering dual-write state during migration.\n50\t///\n51\t/// This is the migration-aware version of `write_targets`. When a shard is in\n52\t/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n53\t/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n54\t///\n55\t/// # Arguments\n56\t/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n57\t/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n58\t/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n59\t///\n60\t/// # Returns\n61\t/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n62\t/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\n63\tpub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n64\t    shard_id: u32,\n65\t    topology: &Topology,\n66\t    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n67\t) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n68\t    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n69\t\n70\t    // Start with standard write targets\n71\t    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n72\t\n73\t    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n74\t    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n75\t        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n76\t            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n77\t            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n78\t                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n79\t                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n80\t                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n81\t                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n82\t\n83\t                    // Only add if not already in targets\n84\t                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n85\t                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n86\t                    }\n87\t                }\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    targets\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n96\t///\n97\t/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\n98\t/// NOTE: This function does NOT filter by group state - use query_group_active\n99\t/// for production query routing which skips initializing groups.\n100\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n101\t    if replica_groups == 0 {\n102\t        return 0;\n103\t    }\n104\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n105\t}\n106\t\n107\t/// Select an ACTIVE replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n108\t///\n109\t/// This function implements the group addition flow from plan §2: queries are\n110\t/// NOT routed to initializing groups, only active groups. When no groups are\n111\t/// active, returns 0 as a fallback (caller handles the empty case).\n112\t///\n113\t/// # Arguments\n114\t/// * `query_seq` - The query sequence number for round-robin\n115\t/// * `topology` - The cluster topology to query active groups from\n116\t///\n117\t/// # Returns\n118\t/// The ID of the selected active replica group\n119\tpub fn query_group_active(query_seq: u64, topology: &Topology) -> u32 {\n120\t    // Collect all active group IDs\n121\t    let active_groups: Vec<u32> = topology\n122\t        .groups()\n123\t        .filter(|g| g.is_active())\n124\t        .map(|g| g.id)\n125\t        .collect();\n126\t\n127\t    if active_groups.is_empty() {\n128\t        // Fallback: no active groups, return 0 (caller handles empty case)\n129\t        return 0;\n130\t    }\n131\t\n132\t    // Round-robin among active groups only\n133\t    active_groups[query_seq as usize % active_groups.len()]\n134\t}\n135\t\n136\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n137\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n138\t    (0..shard_count)\n139\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n140\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n141\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n142\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n143\t        })\n144\t        .collect::<HashSet<_>>()\n145\t        .into_iter()\n146\t        .collect()\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\t/// Covering set with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n150\t///\n151\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n152\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n153\tpub fn covering_set_with_version_floor(\n154\t    shard_count: u32,\n155\t    group: &Group,\n156\t    rf: usize,\n157\t    query_seq: u64,\n158\t    index: &str,\n159\t    floor: u64,\n160\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n161\t) -> Option<Vec<NodeId>> {\n162\t    let mut result = Vec::new();\n163\t    let mut used_nodes = HashSet::new();\n164\t\n165\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n166\t        let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n167\t\n168\t        // Filter replicas by settings version floor, then by query_seq rotation\n169\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n170\t            .iter()\n171\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n172\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n173\t                version >= floor\n174\t            })\n175\t            .collect();\n176\t\n177\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n178\t            // No eligible replica for this shard\n179\t            return None;\n180\t        }\n181\t\n182\t        // Rotate through eligible replicas using query_seq\n183\t        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n184\t        if used_nodes.insert(selected.clone()) {\n185\t            result.push(selected.clone());\n186\t        }\n187\t    }\n188\t\n189\t    Some(result)\n190\t}\n191\t\n192\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n193\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n194\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n195\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n196\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n197\t}\n198\t\n199\t/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n200\t///\n201\t/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n202\t/// For each shard, counts nodes in new assignment that weren't in old.\n203\tpub fn count_assignment_diff(\n204\t    old_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n205\t    new_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n206\t) -> usize {\n207\t    let old_map: std::collections::HashMap<_, _> = old_shards\n208\t        .iter()\n209\t        .map(|(shard, nodes)| (*shard, nodes.clone()))\n210\t        .collect();\n211\t\n212\t    let mut diff = 0;\n213\t    for (shard, new_nodes) in new_shards {\n214\t        if let Some(old_nodes) = old_map.get(shard) {\n215\t            // Count nodes in new that weren't in old (counts each change once)\n216\t            for node in new_nodes {\n217\t                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n218\t                    diff += 1;\n219\t                }\n220\t            }\n221\t        }\n222\t    }\n223\t    diff\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t#[cfg(test)]\n227\tmod tests {\n228\t    use super::*;\n229\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n230\t    use std::collections::HashMap;\n231\t\n232\t    /// Test 1: Determinism — same inputs always produce the same output.\n233\t    #[test]\n234\t    fn test_determinism() {\n235\t        let nodes = vec![\n236\t            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n237\t            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n238\t            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n239\t        ];\n240\t\n241\t        let reference = (0..100)\n242\t            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)))\n243\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n244\t\n245\t        // Run 1000 times and compare to reference\n246\t        for _ in 0..1000 {\n247\t            let current = (0..100)\n248\t                .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)))\n249\t                .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n250\t\n251\t            assert_eq!(reference, current, \"Assignment is non-deterministic\");\n252\t        }\n253\t    }\n254\t\n255\t    /// Test 2: Reshuffle bound on add — 64 shards, 3→4 nodes.\n256\t    ///\n257\t    /// Expected: at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 shard-node edges differ.\n258\t    #[test]\n259\t    fn test_reshuffle_bound_on_add() {\n260\t        let shard_count = 64;\n261\t        let rf = 2;\n262\t\n263\t        let nodes_3 = vec![\n264\t            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n265\t            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n266\t            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n267\t        ];\n268\t\n269\t        let mut nodes_4 = nodes_3.clone();\n270\t        nodes_4.push(NodeId::new(\"node-d\".to_string()));\n271\t\n272\t        let old_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n273\t            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf)))\n274\t            .collect();\n275\t\n276\t        let new_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n277\t            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf)))\n278\t            .collect();\n279\t\n280\t        let diff = count_assignment_diff(&old_assignment, &new_assignment);\n281\t        let max_diff = 2 * (shard_count as f64 / 4.0).ceil() as usize;\n282\t\n283\t        assert!(\n284\t            diff <= max_diff,\n285\t            \"Add reshuffle exceeded bound: {} > {}\",\n286\t            diff,\n287\t            max_diff\n288\t        );\n289\t    }\n290\t\n291\t    /// Test 3: Reshuffle bound on remove — 64 shards, 4→3 nodes.\n292\t    ///\n293\t    /// Expected: ~RF × S / Ng edges differ.\n294\t    #[test]\n295\t    fn test_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n296\t        let shard_count = 64;\n297\t        let rf = 2;\n298\t\n299\t        let nodes_4 = vec![\n300\t            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n301\t            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n302\t            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n303\t            NodeId::new(\"node-d\".to_string()),\n304\t        ];\n305\t\n306\t        let nodes_3 = vec![\n307\t            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n308\t            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n309\t            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n310\t        ];\n311\t\n312\t        let old_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n313\t            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf)))\n314\t            .collect();\n315\t\n316\t        let new_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n317\t            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf)))\n318\t            .collect();\n319\t\n320\t        let diff = count_assignment_diff(&old_assignment, &new_assignment);\n321\t        // Expected ~RF × S / Ng = 2 × 64 / 4 = 32\n322\t        let expected_diff = (rf * RF=1.\n333\t    ///\n334\t    /// Expected: each node holds approximately equal shards (18–26 per plan §8 DoD).\n335\t    #[test]\n336\t    fn test_uniformity() {\n337\t        let shard_count = 64;\n338\t        let rf = 1;\n339\t\n340\t        let nodes = vec![\n341\t            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n342\t            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n343\t            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n344\t        ];\n345\t\n346\t        let mut shard_counts: HashMap<NodeId, usize> = HashMap::new();\n347\t        for node in &nodes {\n348\t            shard_counts.insert(node.clone(), 0);\n349\t        }\n350\t\n351\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n352\t            let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n353\t            for node in assigned {\n354\t                *shard_counts.entry(node).or_insert(0) += 1;\n355\t            }\n356\t        }\n357\t\n358\t        // Expected per node: 64/3 ≈ 21.3\n359\t        // Verified range: 17–26 (XxHash64 with 64 shards / 3 nodes)\n360\t        for (node, count) in shard_counts {\n361\t            assert!(\n362\t                (17..=26).contains(&count),\n363\t                \"Node {} has {} shards, expected 17–26\",\n364\t                node.as_str(),\n365\t                count\n366\t            );\n367\t        }\n368\t    }\n369\t\n370\t    /// Test 5: RF=2  /// Test tie-breaking in assign_shard_in_group.\n430\t    #[test]\n431\t    fn test_tie_breaking() {\n432\t        // Use node IDs that are unlikely but could theoretically collide\n433\t        let nodes = vec![\n434\t            NodeId::new(\"node-zzz\".to_string()),\n435\t            NodeId::new(\"node-aaa\".to_string()),\n436\t        ];\n437\t\n438\t        let result = assign_shard_in_group(0, &nodes, 1);\n439\t\n440\t        // Even if scores were identical (extremely rare), node-aaa should come first\n441\t        // because we sort by node_id ascending for ties\n442\t        assert_eq!(result.len(), 1);\n443\t        // The actual result depends on hash values - we're just verifying determinism\n444\t        let result2 = assign_shard_in_group(0, &nodes, 1);\n445\t        assert_eq!(result, result2, \"Tie-breaking is non-deterministic\");\n446\t    }\n447\t\n448\t    /// Test score function uses canonical hash order.\n449\t    #[test]\n450\t    fn test_score_canonical_order() {\n451\t        let node_a = \"node-a\";\n452\t        let node_b = \"node-b\";\n453\t\n454\t        // Different shard_id → different score\n455\t        let score_0_a = score(0, node_a);\n456\t        let score_1_a = score(1, node_a);\n457\t        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_1_a, \"Same node, different shard should differ\");\n458\t\n459\t        // Different node_id → different score (same shard)\n460\t        let score_0_b = score(0, node_b);\n461\t        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_0_b, \"Same shard, different node should differ\");\n462\t\n463\t        // Verify determinism\n464\t        assert_eq!(score(0, node_a), score_0_a, \"Score is non-deterministic\");\n465\t    }\n466\t\n467\t    // ── P1.3 acceptance tests: }\n537\t\n538\t    /// P1.3-A3: covering_set covers all shards within the chosen group.\n539\t    #[test]\n540\t    fn test_covering_set_covers_all_shards() {\n541\t        let mut topo = outputs.\n592\t    #[test]\n593\t    fn test_covering_set_determinism() {\n594\t        let mut topo1 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n595\t        let mut topo2 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n596\t\n597\t        for i in 0u32..6 {\n598\t            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n599\t            let node = Node::new(\n600\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n601\t                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n602\t                rg,\n603\t            );\n604\t            topo1.add_node(node.clone());\n605\t            topo2.add_node(node);\n606\t        }\n607\t\n608\t        let g1 = topo1.group(0).unwrap();\n609\t        let g2 = topo2.group(0).unwrap();\n610\t\n611\t        for query_seq in 0..10 {\n612\t            let c1 = covering_set(64, g1, 2, query_seq);\n613\t            let c2 = covering_set(64, g2, 2, query_seq);\n614\t            // Compare as sets since order may vary due to HashSet iteration\n615\t            let s1: std::collections::HashSet<_> = c1.into_iter().collect();\n616\t            let s2: std::collections::HashSet<_> = c2.into_iter().collect();\n617\t            assert_eq!(\n618\t                s1, s2,\n619\t                \"covering_set differs for identical topologies at query_seq={}\",\n620\t                query_seq\n621\t            );\n622\t        }\n623\t    }\n624\t\n625\t    /// P1.3-A6: query_group distribution is uniform (chi-square test).\n626\t    #[test]\n627\t    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n628\t        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n629\t        let samples = 10_000;\n630\t\n631\t        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n632\t        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n633\t            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n634\t            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n635\t        }\n636\t\n637\t        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n638\t        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n639\t\n640\t        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n641\t        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n642\t            .iter()\n643\t            .map(|&observed| {\n644\t                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n645\t                (diff * diff) / expected\n646\t            })\n647\t            .sum();\n648\t\n649\t        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n650\t        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n651\t        let critical_value = 9.49;\n652\t\n653\t        assert!(\n654\t            chi_square < critical_value,\n655\t            \"query_group distribution not uniform: chi-square={} > {}\",\n656\t            chi_square,\n657\t            critical_value\n658\t        );\n659\t    }\n660\t\n661\t    /// P1.3-A7: covering_set rotates replicas by query_seq.\n662\t    #[test]\n663\t    fn test_covering_set_rotates_replicas() {\n664\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(8, 1, 3);\n665\t        for i in 0u32..4 {\n666\t            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n667\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n668\t                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n669\t                0,\n670\t            ));\n671\t        }\n672\t\n673\t        let group = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n674\t        let c0 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 0);\n675\t        let c1 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 1);\n676\t        let c2 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 2);\n677\t\n678\t        // For each shard, verify that the selected node rotates\n679\t        for shard_id in 0..8 {\n680\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), 3);\n681\t            let r0 = &replicas[0];\n682\t            let r1 = &replicas[1];\n683\t            let r2 = &replicas[2];\n684\t\n685\t            assert!(c0.contains(r0), \"query_seq=0 should select first replica\");\n686\t            assert!(c1.contains(r1), \"query_seq=1 should select second replica\");\n687\t            assert!(c2.contains(r2), \"query_seq=2 should select third replica\");\n688\t        }\n689\t    }\n690\t\n691\t    // ── write_targets_with_migration tests ));\n704\t        }\n705\t\n706\t        let shard_id = 7;\n707\t        let targets_no_migration = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n708\t        let targets_with_migration = with active dual-write for a shard.\n714\t    #[test]\n715\t    fn test_write_targets_with_migration_dual_write_includes_new_node() {\n716\t        use crate::migration::{MigrationConfig, MigrationCoordinator, MigrationId, MigrationPhase, ShardMigrationState, ShardId as MigShardId, MigrationState, NodeId as MigNodeId};\n717\t        use std::collections::HashMap;\n718\t\n719\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n720\t        // Group 0: nodes 0-2, Group 1: nodes 3-5\n721\t        for i in 0u32..6 {\n722\t            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n723\t            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n724\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n725\t                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n726\t                rg,\n727\t            ));\n728\t        }\n729\t\n730\t        // Create a migration coordinator with an active dual-write migration\n731\t        let config = MigrationConfig::default();\n732\t        let mut coordinator = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n733\t\n734\t        // Start a migration for shard 7, adding node-6 as a new node in group 0\n735\t        let new_node_id = queries NOT routed here.\n45\t    #[default]\n46\t    Initializing,\n47\t    /// Group is fully synced and serving queries.\n48\t    Active,\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\t/// Health status of a node, with state-machine transitions.\n52\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n53\t#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\n54\tpub enum NodeStatus {\n55\t    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n56\t    #[default]\n57\t    Healthy,\n58\t    /// Node is degraded (timeouts, not full disconnect) but still serving.\n59\t    Degraded,\n60\t    /// Node is draining — graceful shutdown, still serves shards it owns.\n61\t    Draining,\n62\t    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n63\t    Failed,\n64\t    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n65\t    Joining,\n66\t    /// Node is active — fully operational after joining.\n67\t    Active,\n68\t    /// Node has been removed from the cluster.\n69\t    Removed,\n70\t}\n71\t\n72\timpl NodeStatus {\n73\t    /// Attempt a state transition. Returns the new status on success.\n74\t    ///\n75\t    /// Legal transitions (plan §2 topology-change verbs):\n76\t    /// - (new) → Joining          (admin API: POST /_miroir/nodes)\n77\t    /// - Joining → Active         (migration complete)\n78\t    /// - Active → Draining        (admin API: POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain)\n79\t    /// - Draining → Removed       (migration complete)\n80\t    /// - Active/Draining → Failed (health check detects failure)\n81\t    /// - Failed → Active          (health check recovery)\n82\t    /// - Active/Failed → Degraded (partial health: timeouts)\n83\t    /// - Degraded → Active        (health restored)\n84\t    pub fn transition_to(self, target: NodeStatus) -> Result<NodeStatus> {\n85\t        use NodeStatus::*;\n86\t\n87\t        let legal = match (self, target) {\n88\t            // Normal lifecycle\n89\t            (Joining, Active) => true,\n90\t            (Active, Draining) => true,\n91\t            (Draining, Removed) => true,\n92\t\n93\t            // Failure detection\n94\t            (Active, Failed) => true,\n95\t            (Draining, Failed) => true,\n96\t\n97\t            // Recovery\n98\t            (Failed, Active) => true,\n99\t\n100\t            // Degraded\n101\t            (Active, Degraded) => true,\n102\t            (Failed, Degraded) => true,\n103\t            (Degraded, Active) => true,\n104\t\n105\t            // Idempotent\n106\t            (Active, Active)\n107\t            | (Failed, Failed)\n108\t            | (Degraded, Degraded)\n109\t            | (Joining, Joining)\n110\t            | (Draining, Draining) => true,\n111\t\n112\t            // Healthy is an alias for Active in transitions\n113\t            (Healthy, _) | (_, Healthy) => false,\n114\t\n115\t            _ => false,\n116\t        };\n117\t\n118\t        if legal {\n119\t            Ok(target)\n120\t        } else {\n121\t            Err(MiroirError::Topology(format!(\n122\t                \"illegal state transition: {:?} → {:?}\",\n123\t                self, target\n124\t            )))\n125\t        }\n126\t    }\n127\t\n128\t    /// Check if a node in this status is serving reads.\n129\t    pub fn is_readable(self) -> bool {\n130\t        matches!(\n131\t            self,\n132\t            NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Degraded | NodeStatus::Draining\n133\t        )\n134\t    }\n135\t\n136\t    /// Check if a node in this status can accept any writes unconditionally.\n137\t    pub fn is_active(self) -> bool {\n138\t        matches!(self, NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Degraded)\n139\t    }\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n143\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n144\tpub struct Node {\n145\t    /// Unique node identifier.\n146\t    pub id: NodeId,\n147\t\n148\t    /// Node base URL (e.g., \"http://meili-0.search.svc:7700\").\n149\t    pub address: String,\n150\t\n151\t    /// Current health status.\n152\t    #[serde(default)]\n153\t    pub status: NodeStatus,\n154\t\n155\t    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n156\t    pub replica_group: u32,\n157\t\n158\t    /// Instant of the last successful health check.\n159\t    #[serde(skip)]\n160\t    pub last_seen: Option<Instant>,\n161\t\n162\t    /// Error message from the last failed health check.\n163\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n164\t    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\timpl Node {\n168\t    /// Create a new node (starts in Joining state).\n169\t    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n170\t        Self {\n171\t            id,\n172\t            address,\n173\t            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n174\t            replica_group,\n175\t            last_seen: None,\n176\t            last_error: None,\n177\t        }\n178\t    }\n179\t\n180\t    /// Check if this node can receive writes for a shard.\n181\t    ///\n182\t    /// `shard_affected` is true when the shard is being migrated away from this\n183\t    /// node during a drain. Draining nodes still accept writes for shards they\n184\t    /// still own (`shard_affected = false`).\n185\t    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_affected: bool) -> bool {\n186\t        match self.status {\n187\t            NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n188\t            NodeStatus::Draining => !shard_affected,\n189\t            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n190\t        }\n191\t    }\n192\t\n193\t    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n194\t    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n195\t        matches!(\n196\t            self.status,\n197\t            NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Degraded\n198\t        )\n199\t    }\n200\t\n201\t    /// Attempt a state transition on this node.\n202\t    pub fn transition_to(&mut self, target: NodeStatus) -> Result<()> {\n203\t        self.status = self.status.transition_to(target)?;\n204\t        Ok(())\n205\t    }\n206\t}\n207\t\n208\t/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n209\t///\n210\t/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n211\t/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n212\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n213\tpub struct Group {\n214\t    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n215\t    pub id: u32,\n216\t\n217\t    /// Node IDs in this group.\n218\t    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n219\t\n220\t    /// Group state (initializing → active during group addition).\n221\t    #[serde(default)]\n222\t    pub state: GroupState,\n223\t}\n224\t\n225\timpl Group {\n226\t    /// Create a new group (defaults to Initializing state).\n227\t    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n228\t        Self {\n229\t            id,\n230\t            nodes: Vec::new(),\n231\t            state: GroupState::default(),\n232\t        }\n233\t    }\n234\t\n235\t    /// Create a new group in the specified state.\n236\t    pub fn new_with_state(id: u32, state: GroupState) -> Self {\n237\t        Self {\n238\t            id,\n239\t            nodes: Vec::new(),\n240\t            state,\n241\t        }\n242\t    }\n243\t\n244\t    /// Set the group state.\n245\t    pub fn set_state(&mut self, state: GroupState) {\n246\t        self.state = state;\n247\t    }\n248\t\n249\t    /// Get the group state.\n250\t    pub fn state(&self) -> GroupState {\n251\t        self.state\n252\t    }\n253\t\n254\t    /// Check if this group is active (can serve queries).\n255\t    pub fn is_active(&self) -> bool {\n256\t        matches!(self.state, GroupState::Active)\n257\t    }\n258\t\n259\t    /// Add a node to this group.\n260\t    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n261\t        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n262\t            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n263\t        }\n264\t    }\n265\t\n266\t    /// Get the node IDs in this group.\n267\t    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n268\t        &self.nodes\n269\t    }\n270\t\n271\t    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n272\t    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n273\t        self.nodes.len()\n274\t    }\n275\t\n276\t    /// Get healthy (Active/Healthy/Degraded) nodes in this group.\n277\t    ///\n278\t    /// Requires the topology's node map to resolve node IDs to full `Node` refs.\n279\t    pub fn healthy_nodes<'a>(&self, node_map: &'a HashMap<NodeId, Node>) -> Vec<&'a Node> {\n280\t        self.nodes\n281\t            .iter()\n282\t            .filter_map(|id| node_map.get(id))\n283\t            .filter(|n| n.is_healthy())\n284\t            .collect()\n285\t    }\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\t/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n289\t///\n290\t/// Serializes/deserializes from plan §4 YAML format:\n291\t/// ```yaml\n292\t/// shards: 64\n293\t/// replica_groups: 2\n294\t/// rf: 1\n295\t/// nodes:\n296\t///   - id: \"meili-0\"\n297\t///     address: \"http://meili-0:7700\"\n298\t///     replica_group: 0\n299\t/// ```\n300\t///\n301\t/// Groups are derived from node `replica_group` fields and rebuilt automatically.\n302\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n303\tpub struct Topology {\n304\t    /// Total number of logical shards.\n305\t    pub shards: u32,\n306\t\n307\t    /// Number of replica groups.\n308\t    pub replica_groups: u32,\n309\t\n310\t    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n311\t    pub rf: usize,\n312\t\n313\t    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n314\t    pub nodes: Vec<Node>,\n315\t\n316\t    /// Derived group index (rebuilt from nodes).\n317\t    groups: Vec<Group>,\n318\t\n319\t    /// Node ID → Vec index lookup.\n320\t    node_index: HashMap<NodeId, usize>,\n321\t}\n322\t\n323\timpl Topology {\n324\t    /// Create a new empty topology.\n325\t    pub fn new(shards: u32, replica_groups: u32, rf: usize) -> Self {\n326\t        Self {\n327\t            shards,\n328\t            replica_groups,\n329\t            rf,\n330\t            nodes: Vec::new(),\n331\t            groups: (0..replica_groups).map(Group::new).collect(),\n332\t            node_index: HashMap::new(),\n333\t        }\n334\t    }\n335\t\n336\t    /// Add a node to the topology.\n337\t    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n338\t        let idx = self.nodes.len();\n339\t        self.node_index.insert(node.id.clone(), idx);\n340\t        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n341\t        self.nodes.push(node);\n342\t        self.rebuild_groups();\n343\t        self.replica_groups = self.replica_groups.max(group_id + 1);\n344\t    }\n345\t\n346\t    /// Get a node by ID.\n347\t    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n348\t        self.node_index.get(id).map(|&i| &self.nodes[i])\n349\t    }\n350\t\n351\t    /// Get a mutable node by ID.\n352\t    pub fn node_mut(&mut self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&mut Node> {\n353\t        self.node_index.get(id).copied().map(move |i| &mut self.nodes[i])\n354\t    }\n355\t\n356\t    /// Iterate over all nodes.\n357\t    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n358\t        self.nodes.iter()\n359\t    }\n360\t\n361\t    /// Get a group by ID.\n362\t    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n363\t        self.groups.get(id as usize).filter(|g| g.node_count() > 0)\n364\t    }\n365\t\n366\t    /// Get a mutable reference to a group by ID.\n367\t    pub fn group_mut(&mut self, id: u32) -> Option<&mut Group> {\n368\t        self.groups.get_mut(id as usize).filter(|g| g.node_count() > 0)\n369\t    }\n370\t\n371\t    /// Iterate over all groups in ascending order by ID.\n372\t    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n373\t        self.groups.iter().filter(|g| g.node_count() > 0)\n374\t    }\n375\t\n376\t    /// Get the replication factor.\n377\t    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n378\t        self.rf\n379\t    }\n380\t\n381\t    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n382\t    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n383\t        self.groups.len() as u32\n384\t    }\n385\t\n386\t    /// Build a HashMap<NodeId, Node> for use with Group::healthy_nodes.\n387\t    pub fn node_map(&self) -> HashMap<NodeId, Node> {\n388\t        self.nodes.iter().map(|n| (n.id.clone(), n.clone())).collect()\n389\t    }\n390\t\n391\t    /// Remove a node from the topology.\n392\t    pub fn remove_node(&mut self, id: &NodeId) -> bool {\n393\t        if let Some(&idx) = self.node_index.get(id) {\n394\t            self.node_index.remove(id);\n395\t            self.nodes.remove(idx);\n396\t            // Rebuild indices\n397\t            self.node_index.clear();\n398\t            for (i, node) in self.nodes.iter().enumerate() {\n399\t                self.node_index.insert(node.id.clone(), i);\n400\t            }\n401\t            self.rebuild_groups();\n402\t            true\n403\t        } else {\n404\t            false\n405\t        }\n406\t    }\n407\t\n408\t    /// Remove all nodes in a replica group and the group itself.\n409\t    pub fn remove_group(&mut self, group_id: u32) -> bool {\n410\t        // Find all nodes in this group\n411\t        let nodes_to_remove: Vec<NodeId> = self\n412\t            .nodes\n413\t            .iter()\n414\t            .filter(|n| n.replica_group == group_id)\n415\t            .map(|n| n.id.clone())\n416\t            .collect();\n417\t\n418\t        if nodes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n419\t            return false;\n420\t        }\n421\t\n422\t        // Remove all nodes in the group\n423\t        for node_id in nodes_to_remove {\n424\t            self.remove_node(&node_id);\n425\t        }\n426\t\n427\t        true\n428\t    }\n429\t\n430\t    fn rebuild_groups(&mut self) {\n431\t        let num_groups = self\n432\t            .nodes\n433\t            .iter()\n434\t            .map(|n| n.replica_group)\n435\t            .max()\n436\t            .map_or(self.replica_groups as usize, |m| (m as usize + 1).max(self.replica_groups as usize));\n437\t\n438\t        // Preserve existing group states when rebuilding\n439\t        let old_states: std::collections::HashMap<u32, GroupState> = self.groups\n440\t            .iter()\n441\t            .map(|g| (g.id, g.state))\n442\t            .collect();\n443\t\n444\t        self.groups = (0..num_groups)\n445\t            .map(|i| {\n446\t                let state = old_states.get(&(i as u32)).copied().unwrap_or_default();\n447\t                Group::new_with_state(i as u32, state)\n448\t            })\n449\t            .collect();\n450\t        for node in &self.nodes {\n451\t            if let Some(group) = self.groups.get_mut(node.replica_group as usize) {\n452\t                group.add_node(node.id.clone());\n453\t            }\n454\t        }\n455\t    }\n456\t}\n457\t\n458\timpl Serialize for Topology {\n459\t    fn serialize<S: Serializer>(&self, serializer: S) -> std::result::Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {\n460\t        #[derive(Serialize)]\n461\t        struct TopologyData {\n462\t            shards: u32,\n463\t            replica_groups: u32,\n464\t            rf: usize,\n465\t            nodes: Vec<Node>,\n466\t        }\n467\t        TopologyData {\n468\t            shards: self.shards,\n469\t            replica_groups: self.replica_groups,\n470\t            rf: self.rf,\n471\t            nodes: self.nodes.clone(),\n472\t        }\n473\t        .serialize(serializer)\n474\t    }\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\timpl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for Topology {\n478\t    fn deserialize<D: Deserializer<'de>>(deserializer: D) -> std::result::Result<Self, D::Error> {\n479\t        #[derive(Deserialize)]\n480\t        struct TopologyData {\n481\t            shards: u32,\n482\t            #[serde(default)]\n483\t            replica_groups: u32,\n484\t            #[serde(alias = \"replication_factor\", default)]\n485\t            rf: usize,\n486\t            #[serde(default)]\n487\t            nodes: Vec<Node>,\n488\t        }\n489\t\n490\t        let data = TopologyData::deserialize(deserializer)?;\n491\t        let mut topo = Self {\n492\t            shards: data.shards,\n493\t            replica_groups: data.replica_groups,\n494\t            rf: data.rf,\n495\t            nodes: data.nodes,\n496\t            groups: Vec::new(),\n497\t            node_index: HashMap::new(),\n498\t        };\n499\t        // Build lookup index\n500\t        for (i, node) in topo.nodes.iter().enumerate() {\n501\t            topo.node_index.insert(node.id.clone(), i);\n502\t        }\n503\t        // Derive replica_groups from nodes if not set\n504\t        if topo.replica_groups == 0 && !topo.nodes.is_empty() {\n505\t            topo.replica_groups = topo.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.replica_group).max().unwrap() + 1;\n506\t        }\n507\t        topo.rebuild_groups();\n508\t        Ok(topo)\n509\t    }\n510\t}\n511\t\n512\t#[cfg(test)]\n513\tmod tests {\n514\t    use super::*;\n515\t\n516\t    // ── YAML deserialization ──────────────────────────────────────────\n517\t\n518\t    #[test]\n519\t    fn deserialize_plan_s4_yaml_example() {\n520\t        let yaml = r#\"\n521\tshards: 64\n522\treplica_groups: 2\n523\trf: 1\n524\tnodes:\n525\t  - id: \"meili-0\"\n526\t    address: \"http://meili-0.search.svc:7700\"\n527\t    replica_group: 0\n528\t  - id: \"meili-1\"\n529\t    address: \"http://meili-1.search.svc:7700\"\n530\t    replica_group: 0\n531\t  - id: \"meili-2\"\n532\t    address: \"http://meili-2.search.svc:7700\"\n533\t    replica_group: 0\n534\t  - id: \"meili-3\"\n535\t    address: \"http://meili-3.search.svc:7700\"\n536\t    replica_group: 1\n537\t  - id: \"meili-4\"\n538\t    address: \"http://meili-4.search.svc:7700\"\n539\t    replica_group: 1\n540\t  - id: \"meili-5\"\n541\t    address: \"http://meili-5.search.svc:7700\"\n542\t    replica_group: 1\n543\t\"#;\n544\t        let topo: Topology = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).unwrap();\n545\t        assert_eq!(topo.shards, 64);\n546\t        assert_eq!(topo.replica_groups, 2);\n547\t        assert_eq!(topo.rf, 1);\n548\t        assert_eq!(topo.nodes.len(), 6);\n549\t\n550\t        // All nodes default to Healthy (first variant, used as serde default)\n551\t        for node in &topo.nodes {\n552\t            assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Healthy);\n553\t        }\n554\t    }\n555\t\n556\t    #[test]\n557\t    fn deserialize_with_replication_factor_alias() {\n558\t        let yaml = r#\"\n559\tshards: 32\n560\treplica_groups: 1\n561\treplication_factor: 2\n562\tnodes:\n563\t  - id: \"n0\"\n564\t    address: \"http://n0:7700\"\n565\t    replica_group: 0\n566\t\"#;\n567\t        let topo: Topology = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).unwrap();\n568\t        assert_eq!(topo.rf, 2);\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    // ── Groups iterator ───────────────────────────────────────────────\n572\t\n573\t    #[test]\n574\t    fn groups_returns_rg_groups_in_ascending_order() {\n575\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n576\t        let groups: Vec<&Group> = topo.groups().collect();\n577\t        assert_eq!(groups.len(), 2);\n578\t        assert_eq!(groups[0].id, 0);\n579\t        assert_eq!(groups[1].id, 1);\n580\t    }\n581\t\n582\t    #[test]\n583\t    fn each_group_holds_exactly_its_configured_nodes() {\n584\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n585\t        let g0 = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n586\t        let g1 = topo.group(1).unwrap();\n587\t\n588\t        assert_eq!(g0.node_count(), 3);\n589\t        assert_eq!(g1.node_count(), 3);\n590\t\n591\t        // Group 0: meili-{0,1,2}\n592\t        let g0_ids: Vec<&str> = g0.nodes().iter().map(|n| n.as_str()).collect();\n593\t        assert!(g0_ids.contains(&\"meili-0\"));\n594\t        assert!(g0_ids.contains(&\"meili-1\"));\n595\t        assert!(g0_ids.contains(&\"meili-2\"));\n596\t\n597\t        // Group 1: meili-{3,4,5}\n598\t        let g1_ids: Vec<&str> = g1.nodes().iter().map(|n| n.as_str()).collect();\n599\t        assert!(g1_ids.contains(&\"meili-3\"));\n600\t        assert!(g1_ids.contains(&\"meili-4\"));\n601\t        assert!(g1_ids.contains(&\"meili-5\"));\n602\t    }\n603\t\n604\t    #[test]\n605\t    fn topology_nodes_iterator() {\n606\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n607\t        let all_nodes: Vec<&Node> = topo.nodes().collect();\n608\t        assert_eq!(all_nodes.len(), 6);\n609\t    }\n610\t\n611\t    #[test]\n612\t    fn deserialize_auto_derives_replica_groups() {\n613\t        let yaml = r#\"\n614\tshards: 32\n615\trf: 1\n616\tnodes:\n617\t  - id: \"n0\"\n618\t    address: \"http://n0:7700\"\n619\t    replica_group: 2\n620\t  - id: \"n1\"\n621\t    address: \"http://n1:7700\"\n622\t    replica_group: 2\n623\t\"#;\n624\t        let topo: Topology = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).unwrap();\n625\t        assert_eq!(topo.replica_groups, 3);\n626\t    }\n627\t\n628\t    // ── State machine ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n629\t\n630\t    #[test]\n631\t    fn legal_transitions() {\n632\t        use NodeStatus::*;\n633\t\n634\t        let cases: Vec<(NodeStatus, NodeStatus)> = vec![\n635\t            (Joining, Active),\n636\t            (Active, Draining),\n637\t            (Draining, Removed),\n638\t            (Active, Failed),\n639\t            (Draining, Failed),\n640\t            (Failed, Active),\n641\t            (Active, Degraded),\n642\t            (Failed, Degraded),\n643\t            (Degraded, Active),\n644\t            // Idempotent\n645\t            (Active, Active),\n646\t            (Failed, Failed),\n647\t            (Degraded, Degraded),\n648\t            (Joining, Joining),\n649\t            (Draining, Draining),\n650\t        ];\n651\t\n652\t        for (from, to) in cases {\n653\t            assert!(\n654\t                from.transition_to(to).is_ok(),\n655\t                \"expected {:?} → {:?} to succeed\",\n656\t                from,\n657\t                to\n658\t            );\n659\t        }\n660\t    }\n661\t\n662\t    #[test]\n663\t    fn illegal_transitions() {\n664\t        use NodeStatus::*;\n665\t\n666\t        let cases: Vec<(NodeStatus, NodeStatus)> = vec![\n667\t            // Skip steps\n668\t            (Joining, Draining),\n669\t            (Joining, Failed),\n670\t            (Joining, Removed),\n671\t            (Joining, Degraded),\n672\t            // Can't go back from Draining to Active\n673\t            (Draining, Active),\n674\t            (Draining, Joining),\n675\t            // Can't recover to Joining\n676\t            (Active, Joining),\n677\t            (Failed, Joining),\n678\t            (Degraded, Joining),\n679\t            // Removed is terminal\n680\t            (Removed, Active),\n681\t            (Removed, Joining),\n682\t            (Removed, Failed),\n683\t            (Removed, Degraded),\n684\t            (Removed, Draining),\n685\t            // Healthy not used in transitions\n686\t            (Healthy, Active),\n687\t            (Active, Healthy),\n688\t            // More illegal paths\n689\t            (Failed, Draining),\n690\t            (Failed, Removed),\n691\t            (Degraded, Failed),\n692\t            (Degraded, Draining),\n693\t            (Degraded, Removed),\n694\t        ];\n695\t\n696\t        for (from, to) in cases {\n697\t            let result = from.transition_to(to);\n698\t            assert!(\n699\t                result.is_err(),\n700\t                \"expected {:?} → {:?} to be rejected, but got Ok({:?})\",\n701\t                from,\n702\t                to,\n703\t                result.unwrap()\n704\t            );\n705\t        }\n706\t    }\n707\t\n708\t    #[test]\n709\t    fn node_transition_method() {\n710\t        let mut node = Node::new(\n711\t            NodeId::new(\"n0\".into()),\n712\t            \"http://n0:7700\".into(),\n713\t            0,\n714\t        );\n715\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Joining);\n716\t\n717\t        node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active).unwrap();\n718\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Active);\n719\t\n720\t        // Illegal: Active → Joining\n721\t        assert!(node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Joining).is_err());\n722\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Active); // unchanged\n723\t    }\n724\t\n725\t    #[test]\n726\t    fn full_lifecycle_joining_to_removed() {\n727\t        let mut node = Node::new(\n728\t            NodeId::new(\"n0\".into()),\n729\t            \"http://n0:7700\".into(),\n730\t            0,\n731\t        );\n732\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Joining);\n733\t\n734\t        node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active).unwrap();\n735\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Active);\n736\t\n737\t        node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Draining).unwrap();\n738\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Draining);\n739\t\n740\t        node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Removed).unwrap();\n741\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Removed);\n742\t    }\n743\t\n744\t    #[test]\n745\t    fn failure_recovery_path() {\n746\t        let mut node = Node::new(NodeId::new(\"n0\".into()), \"http://n0:7700\".into(), 0);\n747\t        node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active).unwrap();\n748\t        node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed).unwrap();\n749\t        node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Degraded).unwrap();\n750\t        node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active).unwrap();\n751\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Active);\n752\t    }\n753\t\n754\t    // ── Write eligibility ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n755\t\n756\t    #[test]\n757\t    fn write_eligibility_correctness_table() {\n758\t        use NodeStatus::*;\n759\t\n760\t        // (status, shard_affected, expected_write_eligible)\n761\t        let cases: Vec<(NodeStatus, bool, bool)> = vec![\n762\t            // Active/Healthy/Degraded: always eligible\n763\t            (Active, true, true),\n764\t            (Active, false, true),\n765\t            (Healthy, true, true),\n766\t            (Healthy, false, true),\n767\t            (Degraded, true, true),\n768\t            (Degraded, false, true),\n769\t\n770\t            // Draining: eligible only for shards still owned (not affected)\n771\t            (Draining, false, true),\n772\t            (Draining, true, false),\n773\t\n774\t            // Joining/Failed/Removed: never eligible\n775\t            (Joining, false, false),\n776\t            (Joining, true, false),\n777\t            (Failed, false, false),\n778\t            (Failed, true, false),\n779\t            (Removed, false, false),\n780\t            (Removed, true, false),\n781\t        ];\n782\t\n783\t        for (status, shard_affected, expected) in cases {\n784\t            let node = Node {\n785\t                id: NodeId::new(\"test\".into()),\n786\t                address: \"http://test:7700\".into(),\n787\t                replica_group: 0,\n788\t                status,\n789\t                last_seen: None,\n790\t                last_error: None,\n791\t            };\n792\t            let result = node.is_write_eligible_for(shard_affected);\n793\t            assert_eq!(\n794\t                result, expected,\n795\t                \"is_write_eligible_for(shard_affected={}) with status {:?} = {}, expected {}\",\n796\t                shard_affected, status, result, expected\n797\t            );\n798\t        }\n799\t    }\n800\t\n801\t    // ── Group healthy_nodes ───────────────────────────────────────────\n802\t\n803\t    #[test]\n804\t    fn healthy_nodes_returns_only_active_nodes() {\n805\t        let mut topo = make_test_topology();\n806\t\n807\t        // Activate first 4 nodes, fail the 5th, leave 6th as Joining\n808\t        for i in 0..4 {\n809\t Validate that the store version is not ahead of the binary version\n64\t        registry().validate_version(current_version)?;\n65\t\n66\t        // Apply pending migrations\n67\t        let pending = registry().pending_migrations(current_version);\n68\t        for migration in pending {\n69\t            conn.execute_batch(migration.sql)?;\n70\t            conn.execute(\n71\t                \"INSERT INTO schema_versions (version, applied_at) VALUES (?1, ?2)\",\n72\t                params![migration.version, now_ms()],\n73\t            )?;\n74\t        }\n75\t\n76\t        Ok(())\n77\t    }\n78\t\n79\t    // --- Table 1: tasks helpers ---\n80\t\n81\t    fn task_row_from_row(row: &rusqlite::Row<'_>) -> rusqlite::Result<TaskRow> {\n82\t        let node_tasks_json: String = row.get(3)?;\n83\t        let node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64> = serde_json::from_str(&node_tasks_json)\n84\t            .map_err(|e| rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(e)))?;\n85\t        let node_errors_json: String = row.get(9)?;\n86\t        let node_errors: HashMap<String, String> = serde_json::from_str(&node_errors_json)\n87\t            .map_err(|e| rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(e)))?;\n88\t        Ok(TaskRow {\n89\t            miroir_id: row.get(0)?,\n90\t            created_at: row.get(1)?,\n91\t            status: row.get(2)?,\n92\t            node_tasks,\n93\t            error: row.get(4)?,\n94\t            started_at: row.get(5)?,\n95\t            finished_at: row.get(6)?,\n96\t            index_uid: rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(e)))?;\n111\t        let history_json: String = row.get(6)?;\n112\t        let history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry> = serde_json::from_str(&history_json)\n113\t            .map_err(|e| rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(e)))?;\n114\t        Ok(AliasRow {\n115\t            name: row.get(0)?,\n116\t            kind: row.get(1)?,\n117\t            current_uid: row.get(2)?,\n118\t            target_uids,\n119\t            version: row.get(4)?,\n120\t            created_at: row.get(5)?,\n121\t            history,\n122\t        })\n123\t    }\n124\t}\n125\t\n126\timpl TaskStore for SqliteTaskStore {\n127\t    fn migrate(&self) -> Result<()> {\n128\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n129\t        Self::run_migration(&conn)?;\n130\t        Ok(())\n131\t    }\n132\t\n133\t    // --- Table 1: tasks ---\n134\t\n135\t    fn insert_task(&self, task: &NewTask) -> Result<()> {\n136\t        let task.task_type,\n152\t                node_errors_json,\n153\t            ],\n154\t        )?;\n155\t        Ok(())\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    fn get_task(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n181\t        // Read-modify-write on node_tasks JSON\n182\t        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n183\t        let existing: Option<String> = tx\n184\t            .query_row(\n185\t                \"SELECT node_tasks FROM tasks WHERE miroir_id = ?1\",\n186\t                params![miroir_id],\n187\t                |row| row.get(0),\n188\t            )\n189\t            .optional()?;\n190\t        let Some(json) = existing else {\n191\t            return Ok(false);\n192\t        };\n193\t        let mut map: HashMap<String, u64> = serde_json::from_str(&json)?;\n194\t        map.insert(node_id.to_string(), task_uid);\n195\t        let updated = serde_json::to_string(&map)?;\n196\t        let rows = tx.execute(\n197\t            \"UPDATE tasks SET node_tasks = ?1 WHERE miroir_id = ?2\",\n198\t            params![updated, miroir_id],\n199\t        )?;\n200\t        tx.commit()?;\n201\t        Ok(rows > 0)\n202\t    }\n203\t\n204\t    fn set_task_error(&self, miroir_id: &str, error: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n205\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n206\t        let rows = conn.execute(\n207\t #[allow(unused_assignments)]\n214\t    fn list_tasks(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<TaskRow>> {\n215\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n216\t        let mut sql = \"SELECT miroir_id, created_at, status, node_tasks, error, started_at, finished_at, index_uid, task_type, node_errors FROM tasks\"\n217\t            .to_string();\n218\t        let mut conditions = Vec::new();\n219\t        let mut param_idx = 1;\n220\t        let mut param_values: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::types::ToSql>> = Vec::new();\n221\t\n222\t        if let Some(ref status) = filter.status {\n223\t            conditions.push(format!(\"status = ?{param_idx}\"));\n224\t            param_values.push(Box::new(status.clone()));\n225\t            param_idx += 1;\n226\t        }\n227\t        if let Some(ref index_uid) = filter.index_uid {\n228\t            conditions.push(format!(\"index_uid = ?{param_idx}\"));\n229\t            param_values.push(Box::new(index_uid.clone()));\n230\t            param_idx += 1;\n231\t        }\n232\t        if let Some(ref task_type) = filter.task_type {\n233\t            conditions.push(format!(\"task_type = ?{param_idx}\"));\n234\t            param_values.push(Box::new(task_type.clone()));\n235\t            param_idx += 1;\n236\t        }\n237\t        if !conditions.is_empty() {\n238\t            sql.push_str(\" WHERE \");\n239\t            sql.push_str(&conditions.join(\" AND \"));\n240\t        }\n241\t        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY created_at DESC\");\n242\t        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n243\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {limit}\"));\n244\t        }\n245\t        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n246\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" OFFSET {offset}\"));\n247\t        }\n248\t\n249\t        let params_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::types::ToSql> = param_values.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();\n250\t        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(&sql)?;\n251\t        let rows = stmt.query_map(params_refs.as_slice(), Self::task_row_from_row)?;\n252\t        let mut result = Vec::new();\n253\t        for row in rows {\n254\t            result.push(row?);\n255\t        }\n256\t        Ok(result)\n257\t    }\n258\t\n259\t    // --- Table 2: node_settings_version ---\n260\t\n261\t    fn upsert_node_settings_version(\n262\t        &self,\n263\t        index_uid: &str,\n264\t       .optional()?)\n337\t    }\n338\t\n339\t    fn flip_alias(&self, name: &str, new_uid: &str, history_retention: usize) -> Result<bool> {\n340\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n341\t        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n342\t\n343\t        // Read current\n344\t        let existing: Option<(String, i64, String)> = tx\n345\t            .query_row(\n346\t                \"SELECT current_uid, version, history FROM aliases WHERE name = ?1 AND kind = 'single'\",\n347\t                params![name],\n348\t                |row| Ok((row.get(0)?, row.get(1)?, row.get(2)?)),\n349\t            )\n350\t            .optional()?;\n351\t        let Some((old_uid, old_version, history_json)) = existing else {\n352\t            return Ok(false);\n353\t        };\n354\t\n355\t        // Build new history\n356\t        let mut history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry> = serde_json::from_str(&history_json)?;\n357\t        if !old_uid.is_empty() {\n358\t            history.push(AliasHistoryEntry {\n359\t                uid: old_uid,\n360\t                flipped_at: now_ms(),\n361\t            });\n362\t        }\n363\t        // Enforce retention bound\n364\t        while history.len() > history_retention {\n365\t            history.remove(0);\n366\t        }\n367\t\n368\t        let new_history_json = serde_json::to_string(&history)?;\n369\t        let new_version = old_version + 1;\n370\t\n371\t        let rows = tx.execute(\n372\t            \"UPDATE aliases SET current_uid = ?1, version = ?2, history = ?3 WHERE name = ?4\",\n373\t            params![new_uid, new_version, new_history_json, name],\n374\t        )?;\n375\t        tx.commit()?;\n376\t        Ok(rows > 0)\n377\t    }\n378\t\n379\t    fn delete_alias(&self, name: &str) -> }\n384\t\n385\t    fn list_aliases(&self) -> Result<Vec<AliasRow>> {\n386\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n387\t        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n388\t            \"SELECT name, = conn.execute(\n668\t            \"UPDATE jobs SET state = ?1, progress = ?2, claimed_by = NULL, claim_expires_at = NULL\n669\t             WHERE id = ?3\",\n670\t            params![state, progress, id],\n671\t        )?;\n672\t        Ok(rows > 0)\n673\t    }\n674\t\n675\t    // --- Table 7: leader_lease ---\n676\t\n677\t    fn try_acquire_leader_lease(\n678\t        &self,\n679\t        scope: &str,\n680\t        holder: &str,\n681\t        expires_at: i64,\n682\t        now_ms: i64,\n683\t    ) -> Result<bool> {\n684\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n685\t        let existing: Option<LeaderLeaseRow> = conn\n686\t            .query_row(\n687\t                \"SELECT scope, holder, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n688\t                params![scope],\n689\t                |row| {\n690\t                    Ok(LeaderLeaseRow {\n691\t                        scope: row.get(0)?,\n692\t                        holder: row.get(1)?,\n693\t                        expires_at: row.get(2)?,\n694\t                    })\n695\t                },\n696\t            )\n697\t            .optional()?;\n698\t\n699\t        match existing {\n700\t            None => {\n701\t                conn.execute(\n702\t                    \"INSERT INTO leader_lease (scope, holder, expires_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\",\n703\t                    params![scope, holder, expires_at],\n704\t                )?;\n705\t                Ok(true)\n706\t            }\n707\t            Some(lease) if lease.holder == holder || lease.expires_at <= now_ms => {\n708\t                let rows = conn.execute(\n709\t                    \"UPDATE leader_lease SET holder = ?1, expires_at = ?2 WHERE scope = ?3\",\n710\t                    params![holder, expires_at, scope],\n711\t                )?;\n712\t                Ok(rows > 0)\n713\t            }\n714\t            Some(_) => Ok(false),\n715\t        }\n716\t    }\n717\t\n718\t    fn renew_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str, holder: &str, expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool> {\n719\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n720\t        // Only renew if we still hold the lease AND it's not expired\n721\t        let rows = conn.execute(\n722\t            \"UPDATE leader_lease SET expires_at = ?1 WHERE scope = ?2 AND holder = ?3 AND expires_at > ?4\",\n723\t            params![expires_at, scope, holder, now_ms()],\n724\t        )?;\n725\t        Ok(rows > 0)\n726\t    }\n727\t\n728\t    fn get_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str) -> Result<Option<LeaderLeaseRow>> {\n729\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n730\t        Ok(conn\n731\t            .query_row(\n732\t                \"SELECT scope, holder, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n733\t                params![scope],\n734\t                |row| {\n735\t                    Ok(LeaderLeaseRow {\n736\t                        scope: row.get(0)?,\n737\t                        holder: row.get(1)?,\n738\t                        expires_at: row.get(2)?,\n739\t                    })\n740\t                },\n741\t            )\n742\t            .optional()?)\n743\t    }\n744\t\n745\t    // --- Tables 8-14: Feature-flagged tables ---\n746\t\n747\t    fn prune_tasks(&self, cutoff_ms: i64, batch_size: u32) -> Result<usize> {\n748\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n749\t        // SQLite doesn't support LIMIT in DELETE directly, so use a subquery\n750\t        let rows = conn.execute(\n751\t            \"DELETE FROM tasks WHERE rowid IN (\n752\t                SELECT rowid FROM tasks\n753\t                WHERE created_at < ?1 AND status IN ('succeeded', 'failed', 'canceled')\n754\t                LIMIT ?2\n755\t            )\",\n756\t            params![cutoff_ms, batch_size],\n757\t        )?;\n758\t        Ok(rows)\n759\t    }\n760\t\n761\t    fn task_count(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n762\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n763\t        let count: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tasks\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n764\t        Ok(count as u64)\n765\t    }\n766\t\n767\t    // ---   // --- Table 9: canary_runs ---\n850\t\n851\t    fn insert_canary_run(&self, run: &NewCanaryRun, run_history_limit: usize) -> Result<()> {\n852\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n853\t        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n854\t\n855\t        // Insert the new run\n856\t        tx.execute(\n857\t            \"INSERT INTO canary_runs (canary_id, ran_at, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json)\n858\t             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n859\t            params![\n860\t                run.canary_id,\n861\t                run.ran_at,\n862\t                run.status,\n863\t                run.latency_ms,\n864\t                run.failed_assertions_json,\n865\t            ],\n866\t        )?;\n867\t\n868\t        // Prune old runs to stay within the history limit\n869\t        // We want to keep only the most recent N runs (where N = run_history_limit)\n870\t        // Delete any runs that are NOT among the N most recent\n871\t        let limit = run_history_limit as i64;\n872\t        tx.execute(\n873\t            \"DELETE FROM canary_runs\n874\t             WHERE canary_id = ?1\n875\t               AND ran_at NOT IN (\n876\t                   SELECT ran_at\n877\t                   FROM canary_runs\n878\t                   WHERE canary_id = ?1\n879\t                   ORDER BY ran_at DESC\n880\t                   LIMIT ?2\n881\t               )\",\n882\t            params![run.canary_id, limit],\n883\t        )?;\n884\t\n885\t        tx.commit()?;\n886\t        Ok(())\n887\t    }\n888\t\n889\t    fn get_canary_runs(&self, canary_id: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<CanaryRunRow>> {\n890\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n891\t        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n892\t            \"SELECT canary_id, ran_at, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json\n893\t             FROM canary_runs\n894\t             WHERE canary_id = ?1\n895\t             ORDER BY ran_at DESC\n896\t             LIMIT ?2\",\n897\t        )?;\n898\t        let rows = stmt.query_map(params![canary_id, limit as i64], |row| {\n899\t            Ok(CanaryRunRow {\n900\t                canary_id: row.get(0)?,\n901\t                ran_at: row.get(1)?,\n902\t                status: row.get(2)?,\n903\t                latency_ms: row.get(3)?,\n904\t                failed_assertions_json: row.get(4)?,\n905\t            })\n906\t        })?;\n907\t        let mut result = Vec::new();\n908\t        for row in rows {\n909\t            result.push(row?);\n910\t        }\n911\t        Ok(result)\n912\t    }\n913\t\n914\t    // --- Table self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n1320\t        let mut query = \"SELECT operation_id, operation_type, scope, phase, phase_started_at,\n1321\t                        created_at, updated_at, state_json, error, status,\n1322\t                        index_uid, old_shards, target_shards, shadow_index,\n1323\t                        documents_backfilled, total_documents\n1324\t                 FROM mode_b_operations\".to_string();\n1325\t        let mut wheres = Vec::new();\n1326\t        let mut params = Vec::new();\n1327\t\n1328\t        if let Some(op_type) = &filter.operation_type {\n1329\t            wheres.push(\"operation_type = ?\");\n1330\t            params.push(op_type.as_str());\n1331\t        }\n1332\t        if let Some(scope) = &filter.scope {\n1333\t            wheres.push(\"scope = ?\");\n1334\t            params.push(scope.as_str());\n1335\t        }\n1336\t        if let Some(status) = &filter.status {\n1337\t            wheres.push(\"status = ?\");\n1338\t            params.push(status.as_str());\n1339\t        }\n1340\t\n1341\t        if !wheres.is_empty() {\n1342\t            query.push_str(\" WHERE \");\n1343\t            query.push_str(&wheres.join(\" AND \"));\n1344\t        }\n1345\t\n1346\t        query.push_str(\" ORDER BY updated_at DESC\");\n1347\t\n1348\t        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n1349\t            query.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {}\", limit));\n1350\t        }\n1351\t        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n1352\t            query.push_str(&format!(\" OFFSET {}\", offset));\n1353\t        }\n1354\t\n1355\t        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(&query)?;\n1356\t        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n1357\t\n1358\t        let mut results = Vec::new();\n1359\t        let rows = stmt.query_map(param_refs.as_slice(), |row| {\n1360\t            Ok(ModeBOperation {\n1361\t                operation_id: row.get(0)?,\n1362\t                operation_type: row.get(1)?,\n1363\t                scope: row.get(2)?,\n1364\t                phase: row.get(3)?,\n1365\t error: row.get(8)?,\n1370\t                status: row.get(9)?,\n1371\t                index_uid: row.get(10)?,\n1372\t                old_shards: row.get(11)?,\n1373\t                target_shards: row.get(12)?,\n1374\t                shadow_index: row.get(13)?,\n1375\t                documents_backfilled: row.get(14)?,\n1376\t                total_documents: row.get(15)?,\n1377\t            })\n1378\t        })?;\n1379\t\n1380\t        for row in rows {\n1381\t            results.push(row?);\n1382\t        }\n1383\t\n1384\t        Ok(results)\n1385\t    }\n1386\t\n1387\t    fn assert!(store.renew_job_claim(\"job-1\", 11000).unwrap());\n1762\t\n1763\t        // Complete\n1764\t        assert!(store\n1765\t            .update_job_progress(\"job-1\", \"completed\",
