Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalAdminRateLimiterInner::default())),\n164\t        }\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Check rate limit and exponential backoff.\n168\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n169\t    pub fn check(\n170\t        &self,\n171\t        ip: &str,\n172\t        limit: u64,\n173\t        window_ms: u64,\n174\t        failed_threshold: u32,\n175\t        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n176\t        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n177\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n178\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n179\t        let now = now_ms();\n180\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n181\t\n182\t        // Check if we're in backoff mode\n183\t        if let Some(backoff_until) = state.backoff_until_ms {\n184\t            if backoff_until > now {\n185\t                let wait_seconds = ((backoff_until - now) / 1000) as u64;\n186\t                return (false, Some(wait_seconds));\n187\t            }\n188\t            // Backoff expired, clear it\n189\t            state.backoff_until_ms = None;\n190\t        }\n191\t\n192\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n193\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n194\t\n195\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n196\t        if state.request_timestamps_ms.len() >= limit as usize {\n197\t            // Enter backoff mode after threshold consecutive failures\n198\t            let failed = state.failed_count + 1;\n199\t            state.failed_count = failed;\n200\t\n201\t            if failed >= failed_threshold {\n202\t                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((failed - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7)); // Cap at 2^7 = 128x\n203\t                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n204\t                state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n205\t                return (false, Some(backoff_seconds));\n206\t            }\n207\t\n208\t            return (false, None);\n209\t        }\n210\t\n211\t        // Record this request\n212\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.push(now);\n213\t        (true, None)\n214\t    }\n215\t\n216\t    /// Reset rate limit and backoff state on successful login.\n217\t    pub fn reset(&self, ip: &str) {\n218\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n219\t        inner.state.remove(ip);\n220\t    }\n221\t\n222\t    /// Record a failed login attempt (for backoff calculation).\n223\t    pub fn record_failure(&self, ip: &str, failed_threshold: u32, backoff_start_minutes: u64, backoff_max_hours: u64) -> Option<u64> {\n224\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n225\t        let now = now_ms();\n226\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n227\t\n228\t        state.failed_count += 1;\n229\t\n230\t        if state.failed_count >= failed_threshold {\n231\t            let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((state.failed_count - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7));\n232\t            let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n233\t            state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n234\t            return Some(backoff_seconds);\n235\t        }\n236\t\n237\t        None\n238\t    }\n239\t}\n240\t\n241\timpl Default for LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n242\t    fn default() -> Self {\n243\t        Self::new()\n244\t    }\n245\t}\n246\t\n247\t/// In-memory rate limiter for search UI (local backend only).\n248\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n249\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n250\tpub struct }\n719\t\n720\t    /// Mark the service as ready (all nodes reachable).\n721\t    pub async fn mark_ready(&self) {\n722\t        *self.ready.write().await = true;\n723\t        info!(\"Service marked as ready\");\n724\t    }\n725\t\n726\t    /// Check if a covering quorum is reachable.\n727\t    pub async fn check_covering_quorum(&self) -> bool {\n728\t        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n729\t        let node_map = topo.node_map();\n730\t\n731\t        // For each replica group, check if we have enough healthy nodes\n732\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n733\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n734\t            let required = (topo.rf() + 1) / 2; // Simple majority for quorum\n735\t            if healthy.len() < required {\n736\t                return false;\n737\t            }\n738\t        }\n739\t\n740\t        true\n741\t    }\n742\t\n743\t    /// Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus (called from health checker).\n744\t    pub async fn sync_rebalancer_metrics_to_prometheus(&self) {\n745\t        if let Some(ref rebalancer) = self.rebalancer {\n746\t            let rebalancer_metrics = rebalancer.metrics.read().await;\n747\t            let in_progress = rebalancer_metrics.rebalance_start_time.is_some();\n748\t            self.metrics.set_rebalance_in_progress(in_progress);\n749\t\n750\t            // Calculate delta for documents migrated counter\n751\t            let current_total = rebalancer_metrics.documents_migrated_total;\n752\t            let previous = self.previous_docs_migrated.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n753\t            if current_total > previous {\n754\t                let delta = current_total - previous;\n755\t                self.metrics.inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(delta);\n756\t                self.previous_docs_migrated.store(current_total, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n757\t            }\n758\t\n759\t            let duration = rebalancer_metrics.current_duration_secs();\n760\t            if duration > 0.0 {\n761\t                self.metrics.observe_rebalance_duration(duration);\n762\t            }\n763\t        }\n764\t    }\n765\t}\n766\t\n767\t/// Response for GET StatusCode>\n798\twhere\n799\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n800\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n801\t{\n802\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n803\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n804\t\n805\t    // Count degraded nodes\n806\t    let degraded_count = topo.nodes().filter(|n| !n.is_healthy()).count() as u32;\n807\t\n808\t    // Check rebalance status\n809\t    let rebalance_in_progress = if let Some(ref rebalancer) = state.rebalancer {\n810\t        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n811\t        status.in_progress\n812\t    } else {\n813\t        false\n814\t    };\n815\t\n816\t    // Build node info list\n817\t    let nodes: Vec<NodeInfo> = topo\n818\t        .nodes()\n819\t        .map(|n| NodeInfo {\n820\t            id: routing table\n824\t            last_seen_ms: 0, // TODO: track last health check time\n825\t            error: None,     // TODO: populate from last health check error\n826\t        })\n827\t        .collect();\n828\t\n829\t    // Check if fully covered\n830\t    let fully_covered = degraded_count == 0;\n831\t\n832\t    let response = TopologyResponse {\n833\t        shards: topo.shards,\n834\t        replication_factor: topo.rf() as u32,\n835\t        nodes,\n836\t        degraded_node_count: degraded_count,\n837\t        rebalance_in_progress,\n838\t        fully_covered,\n839\t    };\n840\t\n841\t    Ok(Json(response))\n842\t}\n843\t\n844\t/// GET /_miroir/shards — shard → node mapping table.\n845\tpub async fn get_shards<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<ShardsResponse>, StatusCode>\n846\twhere\n847\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n848\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n849\t{\n850\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n851\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n852\t    let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n853\t\n854\t    // Build shard -> node mapping using rendezvous hash\n855\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n856\t        let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n857\t\n858\t        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n859\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n860\t            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n861\t            for node_id in assigned {\n862\t                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n863\t            }\n864\t        }\n865\t\n866\t        shards.insert(shard_id.to_string(), node_ids);\n867\t    }\n868\t\n869\t    Ok(Json(ShardsResponse { shards }))\n870\t}\n871\t\n872\t/// GET /_miroir/ready — readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\n873\tpub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\n874\twhere\n875\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n876\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n877\t{\n878\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n879\t    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n880\t\n881\t    if ready {\n882\t        Ok(\"\")\n883\t    } else {\n884\t        // Not yet marked ready - check if covering quorum exists\n885\t        let has_quorum = state.check_covering_quorum().await;\n886\t        if has_quorum {\n887\t            // Auto-mark ready on first successful quorum check\n888\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n889\t            Ok(\"\")\n890\t        } else {\n891\t            Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n892\t        }\n893\t    }\n894\t}\n895\t\n896\t/// GET /_miroir/metrics — admin-key-gated Prometheus metrics.\n897\tpub async fn get_metrics<S>(State(state): state.metrics.encode_metrics() {\n904\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics.into_response(),\n905\t        Err(e) => {\n906\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n907\t            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()\n908\t        }\n909\t    }\n910\t}\n911\t\n912\t/// POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key — manual rotation trigger.\n913\t///\n914\t/// Admin-gated endpoint that initiates a scoped key rotation for the given index.\n915\t/// Set `force: true` in the request body to bypass the timing gate.\n916\tpub async fn rotate_scoped_key_handler<S>(\n917\t    State(state): State<S>,\n918\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n919\t    Json(body): Json<RotateScopedKeyRequest>,\n920\t) -> Result<Json<RotateScopedKeyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)>\n921\twhere\n922\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n923\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n924\t{\n925\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n926\t\n927\t    let redis = app_state.redis_store.clone().ok_or_else(|| {\n928\t        (\n929\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n930\t            \"scoped key rotation requires Redis task store\".into(),\n931\t        )\n932\t    })?;\n933\t\n934\t    if !app_state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n935\t        return Err((\n936\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n937\t            \"search_ui is not enabled\".into(),\n938\t        ));\n939\t    }\n940\t\n941\t    let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n942\t        config: app_state.config.clone(),\n943\t        redis,\n944\t        pod_id: app_state.pod_id.clone(),\n945\t    };\n946\t\n947\t    info!(\n948\t        index = %index,\n949\t        force = body.force,\n950\t        pod_id = %app_state.pod_id,\n951\t        \"manual scoped key rotation triggered\"\n952\t    );\n953\t\n954\t    match scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&rotation_state, &index, body.force).await {\n955\t        Ok(response) => Ok(Json(response)),\n956\t        Err(e) => {\n957\t            error!(index = %index, error = %e, \"manual scoped key rotation failed\");\n958\t            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))\n959\t        }\n960\t    }\n961\t}\n962\t\n963\t/// Parse a rate limit string like \"10/minute\" into (limit, window_seconds).\n964\tpub fn parse_rate_limit(s: &str) -> Result<(u64, u64), String> {\n965\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.split('/').collect();\n966\t    if parts.len() != 2 {\n967\t        return Err(format!(\"invalid rate limit format: '{}', expected 'N/UNIT'\", s));\n968\t    }\n969\t    let limit: u64 = parts[0].parse()\n970\t        .map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid limit number: '{}'\", parts[0]))?;\n971\t    let window_seconds = match parts[1] {\n972\t        \"second\" | \"s\" => 1,\n973\t        \"minute\" | \"m\" => 60,\n974\t        \"hour\" | \"h\" => 3600,\n975\t        \"day\" | \"d\" => 86400,\n976\t        unit => return Err(format!(\"invalid time unit: '{}', expected second/minute/hour/day\", unit)),\n977\t    };\n978\t    Ok((limit, window_seconds))\n979\t}\n980\t\n981\t/// Generate a random session ID.\n982\tfn generate_session_id() -> String {\n983\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 24];\n984\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n985\t    hex::encode(&bytes)\n986\t}\n987\t\n988\t/// POST /_miroir/admin/login — admin login with rate limiting and exponential backoff.\n989\t///\n990\t/// Request body:\n991\t/// ```json\n992\t/// { \"admin_key\": \"...\" }\n993\t/// ```\n994\t///\n995\t/// On success, sets a `miroir_admin_session` cookie and returns:\n996\t/// ```json\n997\t/// { \"success\": true }\n998\t/// ```\n999\t///\n1000\t/// Rate limiting (per source IP):\n1001\t/// - 10 requests per minute (configurable via `admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip`)\n1002\t/// - After 5 consecutive failed attempts, exponential backoff applies:\n1003\t///   - 10m, 20m, 40m, ... up to 24h cap\n1004\t///\n1005\t/// Successful login resets both the rate limit counter and backoff state.\n1006\tpub async fn admin_login<S>(\n1007\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1008\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n1009\t    Json(body): Json<AdminLoginRequest>,\n1010\t) -> Response\n1011\twhere\n1012\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1013\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1014\t{\n1015\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n1016\t\n1017\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n1018\t    let source_ip = headers\n1019\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n1020\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n1021\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n1022\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n1023\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n1024\t        .trim()\n1025\t        .to_string();\n1026\t\n1027\t    // Parse rate limit config\n1028\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n1029\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n1030\t        Err(e) => {\n1031\t            error!(error = %e, \"invalid admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config\");\n1032\t            return (\n1033\t                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1034\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1035\t                    success: false,\n1036\t                    message: Some(\"Rate limit configuration error\".into()),\n1037\t                }),\n1038\t            ).into_response();\n1039\t        }\n1040\t    };\n1041\t\n1042\t    // Check rate limit and backoff\n1043\t    let backend = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n1044\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n1045\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1046\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n1047\t                Ok((allowed, wait_seconds)) => {\n1048\t                    if !allowed {\n1049\t                        if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n1050\t                            warn!(\n1051\t                                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1052\t                                wait_seconds = ws,\n1053\t                                \"admin login rate limited (backoff)\"\n1054\t                            );\n1055\t                            return (\n1056\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n1057\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1058\t                                    success: false,\n1059\t                                    message: Some(format!(\n1060\t                                        \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n1061\t                                        ws\n1062\t                                    )),\n1063\t                                }),\n1064\t                            ).into_response();\n1065\t                        } else {\n1066\t                            return (\n1067\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n1068\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1069\t                                    success: false,\n1070\t                                    message: Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into()),\n1071\t                                }),\n1072\t                            ).into_response();\n1073\t                        }\n1074\t                    }\n1075\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n1076\t                }\n1077\t                Err(e) => {\n1078\t                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to check admin login rate limit\");\n1079\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n1080\t                }\n1081\t            }\n1082\t        }\n1083\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1084\t        // Local backend rate limiting\n1085\t        let (allowed, wait_seconds) = state.local_rate_limiter.check(\n1086\t            &source_ip,\n1087\t            limit,\n1088\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n1089\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold,\n1090\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes,\n1091\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours * 60,\n1092\t        );\n1093\t        if !allowed {\n1094\t            warn!(\n1095\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1096\t                wait_seconds = ?wait_seconds,\n1097\t                \"admin login rate limited (local backend)\"\n1098\t            );\n1099\t            return (\n1100\t                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n1101\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1102\t                    success: false,\n1103\t                    message: if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n1104\t                        Some(format!(\n1105\t                            \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n1106\t                            ws\n1107\t                        ))\n1108\t                    } else {\n1109\t                        Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into())\n1110\t                    },\n1111\t                }),\n1112\t            ).into_response();\n1113\t        }\n1114\t    }\n1115\t\n1116\t    // Verify admin_key (constant-time comparison to prevent timing side-channels)\n1117\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n1118\t    if body.admin_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(state.config.admin.api_key.as_bytes()).into() {\n1119\t        // Successful login - reset rate limit counters\n1120\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n1121\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1122\t                if let Err(e) = redis.reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip) {\n1123\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to reset admin login rate limit\");\n1124\t                }\n1125\t            }\n1126\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1127\t            state.local_rate_limiter.reset(&source_ip);\n1128\t        }\n1129\t\n1130\t        // Generate session ID and seal it\n1131\t        let session_id = generate_session_id();\n1132\t        let sealed = match seal_session(&session_id, &state.seal_key) {\n1133\t            Ok(sealed) => sealed,\n1134\t            Err(e) => {\n1135\t                error!(error = %e, \"failed to seal admin session\");\n1136\t                return (\n1137\t                    StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1138\t                    Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1139\t                        success: false,\n1140\t                        message: Some(\"Failed to create session\".into()),\n1141\t                    }),\n1142\t                ).into_response();\n1143\t            }\n1144\t        };\n1145\t\n1146\t        info!(\n1147\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1148\t            session_prefix = &session_id[..8],\n1149\t            \"admin login successful\"\n1150\t        );\n1151\t\n1152\t        // Set cookie and return success\n1153\t        (\n1154\t            StatusCode::OK,\n1155\t            [\n1156\t                (\"Set-Cookie\", format!(\"{}={}; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/; Max-Age={}\",\n1157\t                    COOKIE_NAME, sealed, state.config.admin_ui.session_ttl_s)),\n1158\t            ],\n1159\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1160\t                success: true,\n1161\t                message: None,\n1162\t            }),\n1163\t        ).into_response()\n1164\t    } else {\n1165\t        // Wrong admin_key - record failure for backoff tracking\n1166\t        warn!(\n1167\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1168\t            \"admin login failed: invalid admin_key\"\n1169\t        );\n1170\t\n1171\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n1172\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1173\t                let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n1174\t                let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n1175\t                let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n1176\t\n1177\t                if let Err(e) = redis.record_failure_admin_login(\n1178\t                    &source_ip,\n1179\t                    failed_threshold,\n1180\t                    backoff_start_minutes,\n1181\t                    backoff_max_hours,\n1182\t                ) {\n1183\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to record admin login failure\");\n1184\t                }\n1185\t            }\n1186\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1187\t            let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n1188\t            let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n1189\t            let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n1190\t\n1191\t            state.local_rate_limiter.record_failure(\n1192\t                &source_ip,\n1193\t                failed_threshold,\n1194\t                backoff_start_minutes,\n1195\t                backoff_max_hours * 60,\n1196\t            );\n1197\t        }\n1198\t\n1199\t        (\n1200\t            StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED,\n1201\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1202\t                success: false,\n1203\t                message: Some(\"Invalid admin key\".into()),\n1204\t            }),\n1205\t        ).into_response()\n1206\t    }\n1207\t}\n1208\t\n1209\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1210\t// Rebalancer Admin API Endpoints (plan §4)\n1211\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1212\t\n1213\t/// POST /_miroir/nodes — Add a node to a replica group.\n1214\t///\n1215\t/// Request body:\n1216\t/// ```json\n1217\t/// {\n1218\t///   \"id\": \"node-new\",\n1219\t///   \"address\": \"http://node-new:7700\",\n1220\t///   \"replica_group\": 0\n1221\t/// }\n1222\t/// ```\n1223\t///\n1224\t/// Implements plan §2 \"Adding a node to an existing group\":\n1225\t/// 1. Add node to topology in `Joining` state\n1226\t/// 2. Send `NodeAdded` event to rebalancer worker\n1227\t/// 3. Worker computes affected shards and starts migration with leader lease\n1228\tpub async fn add_node<S>(\n1229\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1230\t    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n1231\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1232\twhere\n1233\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1234\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1235\t{\n1236\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n1237\t\n1238\t    let id = body.get(\"id\")\n1239\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1240\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'id' field\".into()))?\n1241\t        .to_string();\n1242\t\n1243\t    let address = body.get(\"address\")\n1244\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1245\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'address' field\".into()))?\n1246\t        .to_string();\n1247\t\n1248\t    let replica_group = body.get(\"replica_group\")\n1249\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n1250\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'replica_group' field\".into()))?\n1251\t        as u32;\n1252\t\n1253\t    // Add node to topology\n1254\t    {\n1255\t        let mut topo =    return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n1260\t                format!(\"Node {} already exists\", id)));\n1261\t        }\n1262\t        // Check if replica group exists\n1263\t        let does not exist\", replica_group)));\n1267\t        }\n1268\t        let node = Node::new(node_id, address, replica_group);\n1269\t        topo.add_node(node);\n1270\t    }\n1271\t\n1272\t    // Send         error!(error = %e, node_id = %id, \"failed to send NodeAdded event to rebalancer worker\");\n1281\t            return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1282\t                format!(\"Failed to queue rebalancing: {}\", e)));\n1283\t        }\n1284\t    }\n1285\t\n1286\t    info!(node_id = %id, replica_group, \"Node addition queued for rebalancing\");\n1287\t start shortly\", id, replica_group),\n1291\t    })))\n1292\t}\n1293\t\n1294\t/// DELETE /_miroir/nodes/{id} — Remove a node from the cluster.\n1295\t///\n1296\t/// Request body (optional):\n1297\t/// ```json\n1298\t/// {\n1299\t///   \"force\": false  // Set to true to bypass draining check\n1300\t/// }\n1301\t/// ```\n1302\t///\n1303\t/// Requires the node to be in `draining` state unless `force=true`.\n1304\t/// Note: This only removes the node from topology. Draining must be completed first.\n1305\tpub async fn remove_node<S>(\n1306\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1307\t    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n1308\t    Json(body): .find(|g| g.id == node.replica_group)\n1390\t            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!(\"Replica group {} not found\", node.replica_group)))?;\n1391\t\n1392\t        if   index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n1519\t    };\n1520\t\n1521\t    if let Err(e) = worker.event_sender().try_send(event) {\n1522\t        error!(error = %e, node_id = %node_id, \"failed to send NodeRecovered event to rebalancer worker\");\n1523\t        return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!(\"Failed to queue node recovery: {}\", e)));\n1524\t    }\n1525\t\n1526\t    info!(node_id = %node_id, replica_group, \"Node recovery queued for handling\");\n1527\t    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n1528\t        \"node_id\": node_id,\n1529\t        \"replica_group\": status.\n1535\tpub async fn get_rebalance_status<S>(\n1536\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1537\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1538\twhere\n1539\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1540\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1541\t{\n1542\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n1543\t\n1544\t    // Get rebalancer status if available\n1545\t    let rebalancer_status = /_miroir/replica_groups — Add a replica group.\n1576\t///\n1577\t/// Request body:\n1578\t/// ```json\n1579\t/// {\n1580\t///   \"group_id\": 2,\n1581\t///   \"nodes\": [\n1582\t///     {\"id\": hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Request body for POST /_miroir/admin/login.\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\npub struct AdminLoginRequest {\n    pub admin_key: String,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for AdminLoginRequest {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"AdminLoginRequest\")\n            .field(\"admin_key\",
